rulings.Case Title: CONS. OF PATRICIA ANN FIEBIG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Hard copies have been requested for various documents, but Petitioner states that they will not be provided. 2) Bond must be increased, but Petitioner has refused to file an ex parte petition to increase bond. 3) Attorney requests $9,022.50 in fees, the amount of which was specified in a declaration, and not an amendment, as directed. 4) The starting balance of the accounting does not match the ending balance of the prior account. 5) The receipts schedule shows a "POH Net increase" of $6,445.51. Details are not provided. 6) Petitioner has spent $3,364 on trips to visit the Conservatee. Only a general explanation is provided. 7) Payments of $1,235 costs and $673.12 "legal findings" to Kato, Feder & Suzuki have not been substantiated. 8) The verifications to the petition and the Second Supplement are computer fonts. Petitioner alleges that it is an electronic signature allowed by law. 9) Attorney requests $971.23 in costs. Attorney states that the current description "is sufficient as the Probate Examiner has no authority to set a standard for what is or what is not a sufficient description." 10) The invoices for attorney services only show $4,275 total attorney services, but he requests $6,525. The invoices also show only $1,822.50 in paralegal services, but $2,497.50 is requested. Only a footnote is provided. 11) $624 was paid to "Legal Shield". 12) Petitioner alleges that Wells Fargo Account 6387 has been properly verified because "(a)lthough the letter is dated January 13, 2021 it doesn't mean the balance in the account is as of January 13, 2021. It just means that is when Ms. Wong dated her letter" 13) The most recent statement for US Bank 6343 filed shows that it contained $12,682.84 at the end of the account period, not $12,662.38. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) On Schedule C please explain entry 06/10/2021 Jennifer Croteau 3/2-4/27 for $325.00. 2) On Schedule C under legal costs please file the invoices for all entries except for the 3/18/2020 entry which is based on the 03/16/2020 order. 3) On Schedule C please explain why $322.34 was disbursed to Jennifer Croteau for mileage, tolls, parking when LRSF 14.57 says mileage, parking, cab/rideshare fares, and tolls are absorbed as part of fees. It appears either this needs to be paid back to the trust or deducted from the trustee's fee request. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LORRAINE D. ELLIOTT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. 2) Whether the estate should be distributed in kind. Whether to order a 2-year account under Probate Code 10950. 3) Attorney for Administrators filed a "Hearing Statement for Probate Accounting Status" that is not signed or verified by the administrators. (CRC 7.103). 4) Examiner still has not received the required proposed order. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ERIK MICHAEL BENGTSSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/02/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) As part of the reimbursement request please explain the $1,000.00 paid to Boutin Jones Inc. Please address CRC 7.700. 2) Please explain the email from Tracy Johnson that has the amount of $7,500.00. Please file a receipt in support of this entry or the court may deny this request. 3) The filing fee was $435.00. Administrator is requesting an additional $56.10 in fees when the usual fee is $5.00 to file through file and serve express. Court to determine how to proceed. 4) Please explain the page alleging Erik's part of the bill is $9,500.00 as this is not a valid receipt. Please file a receipts in support of this entry or the court may deny this request. 5) Please explain the Tracy Johnson email alleging $514.79 as the email is not legible. Please file a receipt in support of this entry or the court may deny this request. 6) The request for reimbursement of administrator's spouses' expenses appears to be self-dealings. The court will determine how to proceed. 7) The Liberty Mutual bill for the $152.00 is not a receipt. Please file a receipt in support of this entry or the court may deny this request. 8) The U-Haul receipt is not legible please file a legible copy. 9) When the petition seeks a non-pro rata distribution, it must show the computation on which the proposed distribution is based. Consents of interested beneficiaries must be filed. [LRSF 14.35 I. 6) a. ii.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 2/14/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARK E. RASKIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/4/22. (No earlier availability and at Counsel's request.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: This appears to be an ancillary estate, although not initiated or disclosed as such. Real property, however, passes under California law, or it may be sold in order for the proceeds to be distributed to the sister state personal representative. [Probate Code Section 12541.] (The petitioner was appointed as the administrator of the estate, not of the "Last Will and Testament" of the decedent, who died intestate. The date of the order appointing the administrator was 7/26/21, although the order was not filed until 7/27/21. The petition caption has conflicting language, as no account has been filed as accounting was waived. Rev. & Tax Code Sect. 19513 was repealed in 2018.) It appears that an amendment must be filed, proposing to distribute the real property pursuant to Probate Code Sect. 6401(c), or the proceeds from the sale of the real property to the domiciliary personal representative. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/18/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LEON GOMEZ CHANG AND MARIA C. GOMEZ CHANG REVOCABLE TRUST DATED JUNE 2, 2002
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Invalidate Trust; For Constructive Trust; For Breach Of Trust; To Compel Information; For Financial Elder Abuse; For Unfair And Deceptive
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Matter set for the court to take a first look at the pleadings. Court to determine how to proceed. Unclear who the other child of Robert Gomez Chan "Bobby" is born in 1995 out of wedlock and if they received notice. 2) Respondent filed a "preliminary response." No legal authority cited. Respondent should be prepared at the hearing to cite probate code sections/legal authority that allow for filing a preliminary response. 3) Respondent did not give direct notice to the petitioners and instead only gave notice c/o their attorney. Notice still needs to be given individually to each petitioner. 4) Please file a family tree.
